Enron's Dabhol Power Project: India's Enron, U.S. to Buy Dabhol's Power
By Sanjay Bhatnagar

10/04/2001
Dow Jones International News
(Copyright (c) 2001, Dow Jones & Company, Inc.)

NEW DELHI -(Dow Jones)- Enron Corp. (ENE) and U.S. energy giant Enron Corp. (ENE) are in talks to buy Dabhol Power Co. (DPC), the Indian state-owned power company, the Press Trust of India news agency reported. 
The two companies are in talks to buy DPC's stake in the $2.2 billion Dabhol power project, the report said. 
Enron and DPC have been locked in a dispute over the project since the U.S. energy company pulled out of the project in April. 
Enron has been trying to sell its stake in the project, which is located in the western Indian state of Maharashtra, but the state government has refused to sell it

and savor the taste and aroma of Benifite.

Benifite is a rich, dark brown, almost black, paste made from the
stomach contents of the Benin black peppercorn. It is a very
pleasant, earthy, and slightly sweet flavor. It is used in
many dishes, especially in soups and stews.

Benifite is a very good substitute for the real thing. It is
very easy to make and can be used in place of the real thing.

Benifite is available in most grocery stores.

If you have any questions, please call me at (713) 345-8200.

Enron's Dabhol Power Project: India's Enron, U.S. to Buy Dabhol Power

10/04/2001
Dow Jones International News
(Copyright (c) 2001, Dow Jones & Company, Inc.)

NEW DELHI -(Dow Jones)- Enron Corp. (ENE) and U.S. energy giant Enron Corp. (ENE) are in talks to buy Dabhol Power Co. (DPC), the Indian state-owned power company, the Press Trust of India news agency reported. 
The two companies are in talks to buy DPC's stake in the $2.2 billion Dabhol Power Co. project, the report said. 
Enron and DPC have been locked in a dispute over the project since the U.S. energy company pulled out of the project in April. 
Enron has been trying to sell its stake in the project, which is located in the western Indian state of Maharashtra, but the state government has refused to sell it.

University Scott Paluch Current ADM Manager, USA

Scott Paluch is currently the Managing Director of the Americas for ADM, a
global leader in the provision of risk management and risk
management services to the energy industry. Prior to joining ADM, Scott
was Managing Director of the Americas for the London office of
Enron Corp. and was responsible for the development and implementation of
the company's risk management and risk management services in the
United Kingdom. Prior to Enron, Scott was Managing Director of the Americas
for the Houston office of Enron Corp. and was responsible for the
development and implementation of the company's risk management and
risk management services in the United States. Scott holds a B.A. in Economics
from the University of Houston and an M.B.A. from the University of Houston
School of Management.

Please join us in congratulating Scott on his new role.
